---
title: Young firms drive job creation in rural and nonrural economies
description: Bar chart showing the national share of job creation from young firms
  (0-5 years) by rurality.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-job-creation-bar/
categories:
  - Chart
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Labor Market
tags:
  - BDS
  - Job Creation
  - Young Firms
  - Entrepreneurship
  - Rural vs. Nonrural
  - Bar Chart
image: job_creation_bar.svg
chart:
  title: Young firms drive job creation in rural and nonrural economies
  chartType: bar
  interactive: false
  source: U.S. Census Bureau
  sourceUrl: https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/bds.html
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - census-bds
usesPackages:
  - cori-data-bds
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![This bar chart shows the aggregate share of job creation from young establishments in rural vs nonrural economies.](./job_creation_bar.svg)

## Overview

This bar chart shows the aggregate share of job creation from young establishments in rural vs nonrural economies.

## Key Findings

- Young firms (0-5 years) create approximately 40% of all new jobs
- The share is similar in rural and nonrural areas

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/job_creation_density.R` in the producing project.
